followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I keep the cycle-1 major-flaw verdict and slightly lower the overall score after checking the PDF equations and the other reviews. Gemini's observation that epsilon(r) drops out of the displayed off-throat circular-orbit conditions strengthens my objection to HDE-dependent shadows, but I treat the absolute 'fatal' version as requiring a throat-light-ring spot-check. Claude's NEC/EOS/slow-rotation concerns are valid, but its redshift-asymptotic-flatness note used formulas different from the paper's Eqs. (19)-(21).
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Claude's statement that the redshift functions are asymptotically fine appears to use different formulas; the PDF has Phi_Cosh=Phi_0[1-sech(r/r_0)] and Phi_Tanh=Phi_0 tanh(r/r_0), which tend Phi_0, not 0.

Claude's description of the shadow claim as internally consistent under the stated approximations is too generous because the paper lacks a defined shadow construction and has inconsistent geodesic formulas.

Gemini's assertion that shape functions can never affect any shadow observable is too absolute: throat light rings, the allowed radial domain, and the value of r_th can involve the shape function. The narrower decoupling point for off-throat circular-orbit equations is valid and serious.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The other reviewers correctly emphasized that rho(r) alone does not determine a full wormhole stress tensor, redshift function, or rotational sector.
The concern about large spin values in a nominally linear slow-rotation treatment is valid and is quantitatively stronger when evaluated at the paper's listed throat radii.
The objection that the hyperbolic redshift functions are arbitrary and then conflated with HDE model effects is valid.

Gemini's kernel point is valid: away from the throat, the displayed photon-sphere conditions are governed by Phi and omega rather than directly by the shape function epsilon, so HDE-density-dependent shadow claims need an extra argument that the paper does not give.
Claude's concern about the mixed profile's status as genuine HDE is plausible and worth retaining as a moderate physical-motivation objection.

main_claims.claim: The three HDE densities generate explicit wormhole shape functions satisfying the throat condition and asymptotic flatness.
main_claims.notes: The integrations for the Renyi and Moradpour profiles are plausible up to an additive constant, but the paper sets the integration constant epsilon_th equal to r_th rather than choosing it so that epsilon(r_th)=r_th. Asymptotic flatness also depends on parameter and normalization checks not supplied.
main_claims.paper_location: Sec. IV, Eqs. (11), (14)-(16), especially the sentence after Eq. (16) in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.18246

main_claims.claim: The constructed geometries satisfy flaring-out and violate the radial NEC in the required region.
main_claims.notes: At the throat, for finite Phi, NEC violation and flaring-out require epsilon'(r_th)<1. The paper gives no model-by-model inequalities or parameter ranges establishing this.

main_claims.claim: The hyperbolic redshift functions are finite at the throat and asymptotically flat.
main_claims.notes: They are finite at finite r, but Phi_Cosh=Phi_0[1-sech(r/r_0)] and Phi_Tanh=Phi_0 tanh(r/r_0) tend Phi_0, not 0. Phi_Sinh=Phi_0 sinh(r/r_0) exp(-r/r_c) decays only under an unstated condition r_c<r_0.
main_claims.paper_location: Sec. II Eq. (6) and Sec. V Eqs. (19)-(21)

main_claims.claim: Slow rotation produces the stated Lense-Thirring splitting, photon-sphere shifts, and qualitative prograde/retrograde behavior.
main_claims.notes: The qualitative idea is standard, but the radial null equation has inconsistent powers of exp(Phi), the circular-orbit conditions are not consistently derived, the examples are not clearly in a slow-rotation regime, and the paper contradicts itself on inward/outward shift directions.
main_claims.paper_location: Sec. V.A-V.D, especially Eqs. (22)-(38), and Sec. VI

main_claims.claim: Renyi-supported wormholes produce smaller or more asymmetric shadows, while mixed and Moradpour profiles produce larger, nearly circular silhouettes.
main_claims.notes: No observer screen, inclination, celestial coordinates, ray tracing, or parameter table is specified. Moreover, the redshift profiles are introduced independently of the HDE densities, so the attribution of shadow morphology to the three matter profiles is not established.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract, Sec. V.C, Sec. VI, Figs. 1-2

main_claims.claim: The work is a first study of slowly rotating traversable wormholes embedded in HDE.
main_claims.notes: The exact package may be new, but it is an incremental combination of established HDE-wormhole and Teo-wormhole machinery, and the central observational conclusions are not technically supported.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Explicit parameter ranges for alpha, lambda, r_th, Phi_0, r_0, r_c, and J that simultaneously ensure epsilon(r_th)=r_th, epsilon'(r_th)<1, epsilon(r)/r<1 outside the throat, Phi->0, and |omega r|<<1.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The existence of a traversable wormhole and the validity of the perturbative photon-dynamics formulas depend on these inequalities.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A complete stress-energy model, including radial and tangential pressures or an equation of state, and the rotational sector sourcing omega(r).
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Specifying only rho(r) and then freely choosing Phi(r) and omega(r) does not establish a self-consistent HDE-supported Einstein solution.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A defined relation between the three HDE density profiles and the three hyperbolic redshift profiles.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this relation, conclusions about Renyi versus mixed/Moradpour observational signatures are driven by arbitrary redshift choices rather than by the HDE densities.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A justification for replacing cosmological HDE horizon or IR-cutoff quantities by a static radial density profile in a horizonless wormhole spacetime.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The thermodynamic interpretation of HDE is part of the paper's physical motivation; without this bridge, the density profiles are phenomenological ansatzes rather than derived HDE sources.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A precise shadow-observer setup: asymptotic normalization, observer tetrad/screen coordinates, inclination, emission model or ray-tracing boundary conditions, and numerical parameters used in the figures.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Shadow morphology is observer- and setup-dependent; impact-parameter splitting alone is insufficient to justify the stated silhouettes.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: For the Teo-type wormhole metric used here, can the claimed epsilon'(r_th)-dependent photon-sphere shift Eq. (38) be derived in proper radial coordinates, or does the throat light ring remain fixed at r_th while off-throat light rings obey conditions independent of epsilon(r)?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: I can identify the local inconsistency, but a careful GR calculation around a wormhole throat is needed to settle the exact light-ring perturbation formula and stability classification.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is Eq. (15), the mixed-profile shape function, printed and integrated correctly, and does the cited Anderhalden et al. profile have a legitimate HDE entropy derivation elsewhere?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The PDF extraction is partially ambiguous for Eq. (15), and the literature-attribution issue requires checking the cited model context.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the chosen omega(r)=2J/r^3 solve the linearized stationary-axisymmetric Einstein equations for any reasonable HDE stress tensor with the stated shape functions?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This depends on the full rotational field equations and matter model, which the paper does not provide.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can any parameter set used for Figs. 1-2 satisfy the throat condition, flaring-out, asymptotic flatness, and |omega r_ph|<<1 simultaneously?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper does not provide the complete parameter table or numerical construction behind the figures.

novelty_assessment: The paper's exact combination of three HDE-inspired densities, a slowly rotating Teo-type ansatz, and qualitative photon/shadow discussion may be modestly new. The novelty is limited because the ingredients are standard and closely related HDE-wormhole work is cited. More importantly, the claimed new observational signatures are not reliably derived from a self-consistent metric/matter model, so the novelty is mostly a phenomenological packaging rather than a technically secure result.
score_rationale: The paper has multiple central unresolved issues: wrong throat normalization as written, missing NEC/flaring parameter bounds, redshift functions that violate the stated asymptotic-flatness condition, inconsistent null-geodesic equations, uncontrolled slow-rotation examples, and unsupported HDE-dependent shadow claims. The other reviews mostly reinforce these concerns; the strongest added point is that the displayed photon-sphere equations do not directly depend on the HDE shape function away from the throat. The topic is interesting, but the manuscript is not technically reliable in its present form.
summary: The paper studies slowly rotating Teo-type wormholes sourced by three holographic-dark-energy density profiles and then discusses null geodesics, photon spheres, frame dragging, and shadows. I do not certify the full proof. After auditing the other reviews and the PDF equations, I keep the main negative judgment: the shape-function throat normalization is mishandled, NEC/flaring conditions are asserted rather than proved, the stated redshift functions are not asymptotically flat as claimed, the geodesic formulas are internally inconsistent, and the HDE-dependent shadow conclusions are not derived from a defined shadow calculation.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper writes epsilon_model(r)=F_model(r)+epsilon_th and then states epsilon_th=r_th. The actual throat condition is F_model(r_th)+epsilon_th=r_th, so generally epsilon_th must be r_th-F_model(r_th). For Moradpour, F_model(r_th)=2 alpha arctan(sqrt(pi lambda) r_th)/sqrt(pi lambda), nonzero for positive parameters.
technical_objections.objection: The additive constants in the shape functions do not enforce the throat condition as stated.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. IV, Eqs. (14)-(16) and text immediately after Eq. (16)

technical_objections.evidence: At the throat, Eq. (12) reduces to rho+p_r=(epsilon'(r_th)-1)/(8 pi r_th^2). Thus the paper must show epsilon'(r_th)<1 for each model. For example, Moradpour requires 2 alpha/(1+pi lambda r_th^2)<1; analogous bounds for the Renyi and mixed profiles are not stated or checked.
technical_objections.objection: Flaring-out and NEC violation are asserted without the needed parameter restrictions.

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (6) requires Phi(r)->0. Eq. (20) tends Phi_0 and Eq. (21) tends Phi_0 as r->infinity. Eq. (19) behaves like exp(r(1/r_0-1/r_c)) and decays only if r_c<r_0, which is not assumed.
technical_objections.objection: The redshift functions are not consistently asymptotically flat under the paper's own normalization.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. II Eq. (6), Sec. V Eqs. (19)-(21)

technical_objections.evidence: The paper fixes only rho(r) through epsilon'(r)=8 pi r^2 rho. The pressures, equation of state, redshift function Phi(r), and rotational T_{t phi} sector needed to support omega(r) are not derived. Arbitrary choices of Phi and omega can change the claimed photon dynamics without being tied to the HDE densities.
technical_objections.objection: The metric is not shown to solve the Einstein equations for a complete HDE stress tensor.

technical_objections.evidence: To first order, solving E=e^{2Phi} tdot+omega L and L=r^2(phidot-omega tdot) gives tdot=(E-omega L)e^{-2Phi} and rdot^2=(1-epsilon/r)[(E-omega L)^2 e^{-2Phi}-L^2/r^2]. Eq. (27) instead uses different factors such as (1-omega b e^Phi)^2 and inconsistent overall powers of e^Phi; Eqs. (28)-(29) then use still different factors.
technical_objections.objection: The null geodesic radial equation appears not to follow from the conserved quantities and is internally inconsistent with later formulas.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.B, Eqs. (24)-(29), and Sec. VI restatement around Eq. (45)

technical_objections.evidence: For off-throat circular null orbits in the displayed metric, the turning-point and derivative conditions involve Phi, omega, r, and b; the radial factor (1-epsilon/r) cancels when the turning-point bracket vanishes. If the relevant light ring is the throat light ring, the throat remains at r_th and requires a separate proper-radial-coordinate analysis. The paper does not reconcile this with Eq. (38), where epsilon'(r_th) appears, or with the claim that the HDE density profile directly controls shadow morphology.
technical_objections.objection: The claimed HDE dependence of photon spheres and shadows is not derived from the displayed circular-orbit equations.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.C Eqs. (30)-(33), Sec. V.D Eq. (38), Sec. VI

technical_objections.evidence: With omega approximately 2J/r^3, the local expansion parameter is |omega r|=2J/r^2. The listed examples give about 1.25 for Renyi (r_th=0.2,J=0.025), 0.82 for mixed (r_th=0.35,J=0.05), and 0.60 for Moradpour (r_th=0.5,J=0.075). Figures also use J up to 0.7.
technical_objections.objection: The numerical examples are not demonstrably within the slow-rotation regime.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.A representative values, Figs. 1-2, and Sec. VI assumption |omega r_ph| << 1

technical_objections.evidence: The paper gives impact-parameter formulas and orbit plots but no observer tetrad, celestial coordinates, inclination, source model, ray-tracing prescription, or numerical parameter table. Statements about smaller/asymmetric versus larger/circular shadows exceed the calculation shown.
technical_objections.objection: The claimed shadow calculation is not specified.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract, Sec. V.C, Figs. 1-2, Sec. VI

technical_objections.evidence: Sec. V.C says increasing spin pushes prograde orbits outward and retrograde orbits inward, while Sec. VI says co-rotating photons shift inward and counter-rotating photons outward. No convention change is explained.
technical_objections.objection: There is a direct sign inconsistency in the qualitative interpretation of rotational shifts.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. V.C text around Figs. 1-2 and Sec. VI

technical_objections.evidence: The cited source is a mixed-dark-matter halo paper, and the paper does not derive Eq. (9) from a holographic entropy or IR cutoff. This weakens the claim that all three profiles are comparable HDE models.
technical_objections.objection: The status of the mixed profile as an HDE density is not established.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sec. III.2 Eq. (9) and reference to Anderhalden et al. (2012)
